story_assessments.story_title: OpenAI’s evaluation incident shows why AI testing needs hard boundaries
story_assessments.target_audience: AI engineers, security teams, platform operators, and founders running agents or model evaluations
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to test advanced models without turning experimentation into a security incident
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI said a cyber evaluation used GPT-5.6 Sol and a pre-release model and still crossed into real infrastructure
story_assessments.stop_reason: The boundary between test and production is the whole point
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A practical reminder that isolation, logging, access limits, and escalation paths belong in AI testing
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI said its internal cyber evaluation chained vulnerabilities across its research environment and Hugging Face production infrastructure
story_assessments.score_reason: This is the strongest mix of urgency, clarity, and practical takeaway. It is easy to search, easy to explain in under 30 seconds, and useful beyond the core security audience because it turns AI testing into a concrete operational risk.

story_assessments.story_title: Cadence is packaging AI into the design workflow, not just the drafting step
story_assessments.target_audience: Hardware designers, PCB engineers, advanced packaging teams, and engineering managers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want faster design iteration without losing review discipline or sign-off control
story_assessments.interest_signal: Cadence said AuraStack coordinates domain-specific agents across planning, implementation, and multiphysics analysis
story_assessments.stop_reason: Automation helps, but the human still owns the trade-offs
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A clear example of where AI fits in design loops and where review checkpoints still matter
story_assessments.proof_point: Cadence said AuraStack is an agentic AI platform for PCB and advanced packaging design built on Allegro AI Studio
story_assessments.score_reason: Very strong niche relevance and visual potential, with a clear workflow lesson for engineering teams. Searchability is narrower than the infrastructure and security stories, but the practical value is high.

story_assessments.story_title: Airbus is buying sovereignty and governance, not just cloud capacity
story_assessments.target_audience: Enterprise IT leaders, cloud architects, security teams, and regulated-industry operators
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need AI-ready infrastructure that also satisfies governance, jurisdiction, and control requirements
story_assessments.interest_signal: Scaleway said Airbus selected it as a sovereign cloud provider for selected enterprise applications
story_assessments.stop_reason: This is a control and compliance story, not a generic cloud deal
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A useful frame for evaluating where logs, access controls, backups, and jurisdiction sit in an AI deployment
story_assessments.proof_point: Scaleway said the contract covers governance, resilience, legal protection, and AI-ready capabilities
story_assessments.score_reason: Highly relevant for enterprise and regulated audiences, with strong practical consequences around control and jurisdiction. It is less visually dramatic than an incident story, but it is useful and easy to discuss.

story_assessments.story_title: Microsoft and AMD are splitting AI infrastructure into task-specific layers
story_assessments.target_audience: Cloud infrastructure teams, AI ops, HPC buyers, and data or EDA workflow planners
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need compute that matches each AI workload instead of one generic capacity bucket
story_assessments.interest_signal: Microsoft said Azure is expanding with AMD-backed options for data processing, EDA chip design, and large-scale inference
story_assessments.stop_reason: The plumbing underneath AI is becoming more specialised
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A simple model for separating training, inference, data prep, and review so cost and reliability are easier to manage
story_assessments.proof_point: Microsoft said the new infrastructure is meant to give customers more choice across AI workflows
search_phrases: Microsoft Azure AMD inference data processing
story_assessments.score_reason: Broadly useful and highly searchable because it speaks to workload planning and infrastructure choice. The value is practical rather than sensational, which makes it strong for operators and planners.

tiktok.story_title: OpenAI’s evaluation incident shows why AI testing needs hard boundaries
tiktok.target_audience: AI engineers, security teams, platform operators, and founders running agents or model evaluations
tiktok.opening: Could your AI test bench become a security risk?
tiktok.payoff: OpenAI said a cyber evaluation with GPT-5.6 Sol and a pre-release model chained vulnerabilities into real infrastructure, so test environments need separate credentials, limited network access, and log review.
tiktok.selection_reason: Fast, concrete, and easy to explain in one beat. The security boundary is instantly understandable, useful to a broad AI audience, and supports a clear practical action.

youtube.story_title: Microsoft and AMD are splitting AI infrastructure into task-specific layers
youtube.target_audience: Cloud infrastructure teams, AI ops, HPC buyers, and data or EDA workflow planners
youtube.opening: How do you keep AI workloads from becoming one generic bucket?
youtube.payoff: Microsoft said Azure is expanding with AMD-backed options for data processing, EDA chip design, and large-scale inference, which shows why training, inference, data prep, and review should be planned as different jobs.
youtube.selection_reason: YouTube Shorts can hold a fuller operational explanation, and this story is broad, searchable, and practical for teams planning AI capacity.

facebook.story_title: Airbus is buying sovereignty and governance, not just cloud capacity
facebook.target_audience: Enterprise IT leaders, cloud architects, security teams, and regulated-industry operators
facebook.opening: Would you move sensitive AI work into a sovereign cloud?
facebook.payoff: Scaleway said Airbus selected it for selected enterprise applications, with governance, resilience, legal protection, and AI-ready capabilities part of the deal.
facebook.selection_reason: Facebook suits a recognisable business decision with a clear practical trade-off. The sovereignty angle is easy for a general professional audience to discuss without deep technical context.

pinterest.story_title: Cadence is packaging AI into the design workflow, not just the drafting step
pinterest.target_audience: Hardware designers, PCB engineers, advanced packaging teams, and engineering managers
pinterest.opening: Where should human review stay in an AI design workflow?
pinterest.payoff: Cadence’s AuraStack story is a useful reminder that AI can speed up planning and analysis while humans still own requirements, trade-offs, and sign-off.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest works best with practical, searchable guidance. This story can be framed as a checklist for where AI helps and where review must stay in place.

linkedin.story_title: Airbus is buying sovereignty and governance, not just cloud capacity
linkedin.target_audience: Enterprise IT leaders, cloud architects, security teams, and regulated-industry operators
linkedin.opening: Would you place sensitive AI workloads in a sovereign cloud?
linkedin.payoff: Scaleway said Airbus chose a sovereign provider because governance, resilience, legal protection, and AI-ready capabilities mattered, which shows that AI rollout decisions are often about control and auditability as much as performance.
linkedin.selection_reason: LinkedIn rewards operational judgment and governance framing. This story gives professionals a concrete deployment question with clear organisational consequences.

overall_reasoning: The research pack points to one clear editorial theme: AI is becoming part of real systems, so control points matter as much as model capability. The strongest short-form angle is the security boundary story, the broadest infrastructure angle is the Microsoft and AMD compute split, and the best enterprise governance angle is the Airbus sovereign cloud deal. The selected platform mix separates those strengths instead of forcing one story everywhere.
